title: After 40 days, Trump's shifting Iran strategy ends in uncertainty and a fragile ceasefire
subhead: The goals of the war launched by the US and Israel against Iran have changed repeatedly over the weeks. While both allies initially counted on regime change in Tehran, for now, they have to settle for merely weakening Iran's offensive capabilities.

title: Trump blasts NATO after closed-door meeting with Secretary-General Mark Rutte
subhead: The US president accused its allies of 'turning their back' on the American people after Washington's partners refused to back his war on Iran. After his meeting on Wednesday, he attacked NATO on social media and appeared to renew threats over Greenland.

title: The exhausting 'racial burden' for victims of everyday racism
subhead: Inspired by the concept of 'double consciousness' described by W. E. B. Du Bois, the American pioneer of anti-racist thought, the phrase 'racial burden' has been circulating in the French media for about a decade. It refers to the state of hypervigilance experienced by victims of racism, who are constantly anticipating and defusing everyday microaggressions.

title: 'Iran is caught in a vice between two regimes of silence: War and repression'
subhead: Franco-Iranian anthropologist Fariba Adelkhah denounces those who rejoice at the weakening of the Iranian regime without acknowledging the violence of war and the dangers posed by contempt for international law and foreign interference.

title: The French government's fear: Appearing impotent
subhead: What story can be told to the French when everything seems to be slipping away, both the conduct of international policy and of domestic affairs?

'He said if the kids pulled off the job, there'd be many more': The strange foiled attack on Bank of America in Paris
subhead: Three teenagers and a 21-year-old man were placed in detention as part of the investigation into the attempted bombing of Bank of America's office in Paris. Investigators suspect that Iranian intelligence services were behind the plot.

title: Are Trump's ideologues intellectuals or imposters?
subhead: Curtis Yarvin has become a leading figure in the Neo-Reactionary movement (also known as the Dark Enlightenment), which has now made it to the White House. But can this blogger really be considered an intellectual? Political theorist Arnaud Miranda and sociologist Olivier Alexandre debate the origins and roles of one of the thinkers who has Trump's ear.

title: Israel's rule of law and democracy are on the brink of collapse
subhead: The introduction of the death penalty for Palestinians convicted of 'a terrorist act,' but not for Israeli Jews guilty of abuses and the murder of civilians in the occupied West Bank, is emblematic of the ongoing transformation of Israel.
